description: Shared Quiz Components & Quiz Engine Consolidation - Extract shared UI components, hooks, and consolidate four quiz screens into a single configurable QuizEngine

userStories.title: Create shared Button component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable Button component so that all buttons across quiz screens have consistent styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/Button.tsx with API: label, onPress, variant ('primary' | 'secondary' | 'danger'), disabled?, testID?, style?
Primary variant uses current submit button styling (shadow, border radius, min touch target)
Secondary variant uses outlined/lighter style for 'Mark as Correct' type actions
Danger variant uses COLORS.feedback.incorrect background
All buttons respect MIN_TOUCH_TARGET from theme
Disabled state has reduced opacity and ignores presses
Unit tests verify: rendering with each variant, disabled state prevents onPress, testID is forwarded
All existing tests pass
All quality checks pass

userStories.title: Create shared SubjectDisplay component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single SubjectDisplay component for the colored subject box so that all screens show subjects with identical dimensions and styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/SubjectDisplay.tsx with API: subjectType, displayMode ('characters' | 'meaning'), displayText, feedbackState? ('correct' | 'incorrect' | 'fuzzyMatch' | null), srsBadge?, subjectTypeLabel?, style?
Container has fixed height of 232px for all modes
Background color comes from getSubjectColor(subjectType)
When feedbackState is set, background changes to feedback color and a 'Correct!' / 'Incorrect' / 'Close Enough!' label appears
When srsBadge is provided, shows SrsLevelBadge or AnimatedSrsLevelBadge in top-right corner
When displayMode is 'meaning', uses appropriate font size for English text
When displayMode is 'characters', uses FONT_SIZES.display for Japanese text
Unit tests verify: correct background color per subject type, all feedback states render correct label and color, SRS badge renders when provided, both display modes render correctly
All existing tests pass
All quality checks pass

userStories.title: Create shared QuestionTypeLabel component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a QuestionTypeLabel component so that the MEANING/READING/KANJI label bar is consistent across all screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/QuestionTypeLabel.tsx with API: type ('meaning' | 'reading' | 'kanji')
'MEANING' uses dark/black background, 'READING' uses distinct background (current reading style), 'KANJI' uses the style currently used in ReversePracticeScreen
Text is uppercase, centered, styled consistently with existing screens
Unit tests verify: correct label text and background for each type
All existing tests pass
All quality checks pass

userStories.title: Create useShakeAnimation hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a useShakeAnimation hook so that the input shake animation on invalid submissions is defined once and reused.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/hooks/useShakeAnimation.ts returning { shakeStyle, triggerShake }
Animation sequence matches current implementation: 5-step timing (10, -10, 10, -10, 0) at 50ms each
Uses useNativeDriver: true
The returned shakeStyle contains transform: [{ translateX }] to apply to any Animated.View
Unit tests verify: triggerShake can be called without errors, hook returns expected shape
All existing tests pass
All quality checks pass

userStories.title: Create useAutoFocus hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a useAutoFocus hook so that the repeated auto-focus-on-input logic is defined once.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/hooks/useAutoFocus.ts accepting a ref and an array of dependencies that determine when to re-focus
Focuses the input after a 100ms delay (matching current behavior)
Cleans up the timer on unmount
Unit tests verify: hook can be instantiated, cleanup runs on unmount
All existing tests pass
All quality checks pass

userStories.title: Create useQuestionInput hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a useQuestionInput hook that handles text input logic (romaji-to-hiragana conversion for reading, plain text for meaning) so input handling is consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/hooks/useQuestionInput.ts with API: questionType ('meaning' | 'reading' | 'reverse')
Returns: { inputValue, displayValue, setInputValue, clearInput, handleTextChange }
When questionType is 'reading': converts romaji to hiragana using existing processRomajiInput/romajiToHiragana utils, displayValue shows converted hiragana + pending romaji
When questionType is 'meaning': passes text through directly
When questionType is 'reverse': passes text through directly (Japanese keyboard input)
clearInput resets all internal state
Unit tests verify: romaji conversion for reading mode, passthrough for meaning mode, clearInput resets state
All existing tests pass
All quality checks pass

userStories.title: Create shared IncorrectFeedbackView component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single IncorrectFeedbackView component so that the incorrect answer screen is identical across all quiz modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/IncorrectFeedbackView.tsx with API: subjectType, displayText, displayMode, userAnswer, correctAnswer, mnemonic, mnemonicLabel, onContinue, onMarkCorrect?, onAddSynonym?, detailsContent?, detailsResetKey?
Uses SubjectDisplay (US-002) with feedbackState='incorrect' for the header
Uses Button (US-001) for Continue and Mark as Correct buttons
Layout: SubjectDisplay at top, ScrollView with feedback sections, buttons at bottom
Feedback sections show: 'Your Answer' (red), 'Correct Answer' (green), mnemonic, expandable details
When onMarkCorrect is not provided, Continue button takes full width
When onMarkCorrect is provided, buttons appear side by side
Unit tests verify: renders all feedback sections, buttons appear/hide based on props, onContinue fires on press, onMarkCorrect fires when provided
All existing tests pass
All quality checks pass

userStories.title: Create shared CorrectFeedbackView component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a CorrectFeedbackView component for the brief correct/fuzzy-match feedback so that it is consistent across screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/CorrectFeedbackView.tsx with API: subjectType, displayText, displayMode, feedbackState ('correct' | 'fuzzyMatch'), srsBadge?, questionType, inputValue
Uses SubjectDisplay (US-002) with the given feedbackState
Shows the QuestionTypeLabel (US-003) and a disabled input with the user's answer
Unit tests verify: correct and fuzzyMatch states render different labels, SRS badge shown when provided
All existing tests pass
All quality checks pass

userStories.title: Create shared ProgressHeader component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a ProgressHeader component that shows a progress bar, zen-mode banner, practice-phrase banner, or nothing depending on quiz mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/ProgressHeader.tsx with API: mode ('progress' | 'zen' | 'practice' | 'none')
For 'progress' mode: accepts current, total, wrapUpRemaining? props and shows progress bar with count/percentage
Wrap-up variant shows 'Wrapping up: X remaining' text
Zen mode shows the 'Zen Mode' label
Practice mode shows motivational phrase with icon (accepts phrase and icon props)
None mode renders nothing
Unit tests verify: each mode renders expected content, progress calculation is correct
All existing tests pass
All quality checks pass

userStories.title: Create shared LoadingView component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a LoadingView component for the repeated loading/activity-indicator pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/LoadingView.tsx with API: message? (defaults to 'Loading...')
Centers an ActivityIndicator with the message text
Uses theme colors consistently
Unit tests verify: renders with default message, renders with custom message
All existing tests pass
All quality checks pass

userStories.title: Integrate shared components into ReviewSession
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to refactor ReviewSession to use all new shared components so that it no longer contains duplicated UI code.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace inline character container with SubjectDisplay (height 232px)
Replace inline question type label with QuestionTypeLabel
Replace inline submit/continue/mark-correct buttons with Button
Replace inline incorrect feedback view with IncorrectFeedbackView
Replace inline correct feedback rendering with CorrectFeedbackView
Replace inline progress bar with ProgressHeader
Replace inline loading state with LoadingView
Replace inline shake animation with useShakeAnimation hook
Replace inline auto-focus logic with useAutoFocus hook
Replace inline input handling with useQuestionInput hook
All existing ReviewSession tests pass without modification (or with minimal test-ID adjustments)
Visual output is identical to current implementation (except height normalized to 232px)
All quality checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

acceptanceCriteria: Replace inline meaning container with SubjectDisplay (height 232px, displayMode='meaning')
Replace inline question type label with QuestionTypeLabel (type 'kanji')

userStories.title: Define unified Question type and answer validation module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a unified Question type and centralized answer validation so that the QuizEngine has a single contract for all quiz modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/quiz/types.ts with unified Question type that all four screens can map their questions into
Create src/components/quiz/answerValidation.ts consolidating meaning validation (with fuzzy match), reading validation, and reverse validation (no fuzzy match, rejects romaji)
Unified Question type includes: id, subjectId, subjectType, displayText, displayMode, correctAnswers, questionType, mnemonic, mnemonicLabel, userSynonyms, auxiliaryMeanings
Answer validation functions accept a Question and user input, return a result with correct/incorrect/fuzzyMatch status
Unit tests verify: meaning validation with fuzzy match, reading validation, reverse validation rejects romaji, synonym matching, auxiliary meaning handling
All existing tests pass
All quality checks pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-011 through US-014 being complete so existing screens use shared components

userStories.title: Create QuizEngine component with queue management
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a QuizEngine component that handles the quiz loop (question display, answer submission, feedback, queue management) configured via props.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/quiz/QuizEngine.tsx with configuration-driven API (QuizEngineConfig type)

Config supports: questions, questionFormat, progressMode, completionMode, allowMarkCorrect, allowAddSynonym, allowWrapUp, requeueIncorrect, showSrsBadge, showSubjectTypeLabel, autoRefill, onAnswer, onComplete, onComponentPress, practicePhrases, practiceIcon, renderCompletion

QuizEngine internally uses all shared components (SubjectDisplay, QuestionTypeLabel, Button, IncorrectFeedbackView, CorrectFeedbackView, ProgressHeader, LoadingView) and hooks (useShakeAnimation, useAutoFocus, useQuestionInput)
Buffer cap logic (MAX_INCOMPLETE_ITEMS) built in, activated only when requeueIncorrect is true
Auto-refill logic triggers when autoRefill config is provided and queue drops below threshold
Re-queues incorrect answers when requeueIncorrect is true
Unit tests verify: correct rendering for key config combinations, answer submission flows, re-queuing behavior, auto-refill triggers, completion callback fires
All existing tests pass
All quality checks pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-015 (unified types and validation)

userStories.title: Migrate ReviewSession to QuizEngine
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ReviewSession to use QuizEngine internally so that all quiz logic lives in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: ReviewSession becomes a thin wrapper that: loads review items, generates questions, configures QuizEngine with review-specific settings (SRS tracking, wrap-up, mark-correct, add-synonym, progress bar, re-queue)
SRS level tracking (level-up/down animations) handled via onAnswer callback and passed to QuizEngine via showSrsBadge
Wrap-up mode managed by ReviewSession and communicated to QuizEngine
ReviewCompletion rendered via renderCompletion prop
All existing ReviewSession tests pass
Behavior is identical to current implementation
All quality checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
